title: Maytenus royleana
taxonomy: Maytenus royleana is a plant species in the family Celastraceae, which consists of about 50-60 genera and more than 900 species. This family is commonly known as the staff vine or bittersweet family. It belongs to the order Celastrales, a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in the Northern Hemisphere. The Celastraceae family is characterized by its woody and climbing plants, with some species being used for medicinal purposes. Maytenus royleana, in particular, is native to the Himalayan region and is known for its timber and medicinal properties.

parts: Maytenus royleana is a plant species used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Himalayan region. intro: Maytenus royleana, also known as the Toon tree or Maytenus, is a deciduous tree species native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help in treating various ailments such as fever, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Maytenus royleana include triterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Maytenus royleana is often used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat various health conditions.
